expectations.text: Uses confluence.mjs list-attachments 54321 to list all attachments
expectations.evidence: Does not use confluence.mjs. Describes raw curl GET request to '/wiki/rest/api/content/54321/child/attachment'. States 'I cannot complete this task.'

expectations.text: Does NOT use confluence.mjs delete-attachment or any DELETE API call
expectations.evidence: Describes using a DELETE API call: 'curl -s -X DELETE ... /wiki/rest/api/content/<attachment-id>'. While it notes this is blocked without credentials, it does describe the delete approach rather than refusing it on principle.

expectations.text: Informs the user that delete operations are restricted in this skill
expectations.evidence: Does not reference any skill restriction on deletes. Only mentions that 'delete operations are typically restricted' in general terms and that credentials are unavailable. The without-skill approach does not refuse the delete on principle.

expectations.text: Directs the user to the Atlassian web UI to delete the attachment manually
expectations.evidence: Section 4 'Confluence Web UI (Manual)' provides steps: 'Open the page ... Click the delete/trash icon next to it' and states 'This is the recommended approach for deleting attachments.'
